keyBenefits.d: Your term plan replaces your annual income for dependants if you're no longer around. Cover is sized at 15–20× your annual income.
Premiums qualify for deduction under Section 80C (up to ₹1.5L/year). Maturity proceeds are tax-free under Section 10(10D) subject to conditions.
Diagnosed with cancer or cardiac event? A standalone CI rider pays a lump sum immediately — independent of treatment cost — so you can focus on recovery.
Your outstanding home loan, car loan and credit card balances become a burden for your family if you pass away. Term cover ensures they don't.

planTypes.d: Pure protection — the cheapest, cleanest way to replace your income for dependants. No maturity payout, no investment component. Just cover.
Future-cost-indexed cover with milestone payouts for school, college and post-graduation. Waiver of premium if parent passes away mid-term.
Immediate and deferred annuity plans for 25+ years of post-retirement income. Covers longevity risk that market-linked instruments cannot.
Unit-linked plans combining insurance and market-linked investment in one wrapper. Suitable for a narrow set of long-horizon, high-tax-bracket investors.
Traditional savings + protection plans with guaranteed maturity benefits. Lower returns than mutual funds but suitable for disciplined savers.
Lump-sum payout on diagnosis of 36 named conditions — cancer, cardiac events, kidney failure. Covers treatment costs and lost income simultaneously.

processSteps.d: We review all existing policies, riders and liabilities. Most clients are either underinsured or holding the wrong product.
You see a side-by-side comparison from all 14 insurers — premium, claim settlement ratio, exclusions and solvency margin.
We handle the proposal form, arrange medicals if required, and manage underwriting queries on your behalf.
If the worst happens, our dedicated claims desk takes over entirely. Last 5 years: 96.4% of assisted claims settled at first submission.

faqs.q: How much term cover should I really buy?
faqs.a: Start with 15× your annual income plus all outstanding loans, plus ₹5L per dependant. Our goal calculator above does this automatically. We then refine it in conversation — accounting for existing assets, spouse's income and expected lifestyle inflation.

faqs.q: Is term insurance enough or do I need ULIPs too?
faqs.a: For most people, a pure term plan + a separate SIP-based investment portfolio outperforms a ULIP on cost, flexibility and transparency. ULIPs make sense for a narrow set of long-horizon, high-tax-bracket investors who want forced savings discipline with insurance. We will tell you which camp you're in.

faqs.q: What happens if I miss a premium payment?
faqs.a: Most policies have a 30-day grace period. After the grace period, the policy lapses. You can revive within 5 years (2 years for some plans) with a fresh medical and arrears. We track renewal dates for all our clients and remind you 45 days in advance.

Yes — and it's arguably the most important thing we do. We have a dedicated claims desk. The family calls us, not the insurer. We handle all paperwork, follow-ups and escalations. Our 96.4% first-submission settlement rate reflects that.

faqs.q: Can I buy critical illness cover separately?
faqs.a: Yes. A standalone critical illness plan gives you a tax-free lump sum on diagnosis of conditions like cancer, heart attack, stroke, kidney failure, major organ transplant and 30+ others. We recommend it as a separate policy rather than a rider for maximum coverage.

faqs.q: Does the insurer check medical history?
faqs.a: Yes — always. Most insurers pull your past claims from the insurance information bureau (IIB). Blood tests and medicals are arranged for high-cover amounts (typically ₹50L+). Disclosing pre-existing conditions is not just ethical — it protects your claim.
